title: thymus serpyllum subsp. nervosus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. nervosus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and pink or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of wild thyme include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic qualities, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin infections. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, sore throats, and coughs. Wild thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
